title: SAK Phase-4 advisory->blocking flip state (sak-state/v1)
description: Validates the hand-maintained SAK-STATE.json. The normative authority for the state machine is plan 033 v7 § 14.4.shadow (the lifecycle diagram), § 14.4c + plan 048 v8 Delta 3 (the advisory->blocking flip exit preconditions: (a) coverage calendar-dispositionable, (c) zero-open-P0 HARD, (d) governance-triple sign-off HARD), § 14.4.rollback (rollback protocol + ROLLED-BACK semantics), and plan 048 v8 Delta 4 / § 14.31 (lifecycle-states). This is DISTINCT from the 13-entity runtime state machine at specs/state-machines/transition-table.v1.json (Blueprint B). This schema is consumed by scripts/sak-state-machine.py (transition emit/validation) and is compatible with the SAK-STATE.json shape that scripts/render-sak-dashboard.py already reads.

as_of.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the slow-moving facts in this file were last refreshed.

lifecycleState.description: The eight states of the SAK Phase-4 advisory->blocking flip lifecycle (plan 033 v7 § 14.4.shadow). The five named in the prompt brief (ADVISORY, ADVISORY-W-A, SHADOW-MODE, READY-TO-FLIP, BLOCKING) plus the canonical diagram's ADVISORY-W-AB (both waves done) and HOLDING (audit branch off SHADOW-MODE), plus ROLLED-BACK (reachable from the gated states).

preconditions.description: The advisory->blocking flip exit preconditions (plan 048 v8 Delta 3, revising plan 033 § 14.4c). Used to gate the READY-TO-FLIP -> BLOCKING transition. (a) coverage is the only precondition the 30-day calendar ceiling can disposition (flip with the long tail quarantined); (c) zero-open-P0 and (d) governance-triple sign-off are HARD gates the ceiling never overrides.

coverage_corpus_pass_pct.description: Precondition (a): fraction of corpus passing the kernel schema in advisory + shadow modes, as a percentage. >= 99.5 satisfies (a); below it the flip MAY still fire only via the 30-day calendar ceiling with the long tail quarantined.

shadow_days_complete.description: Precondition (b): calendar days of shadow mode completed. >= 7 with deviation < 0.5% satisfies (b) (plan 033 § 14.4b.5).

shadow_deviation_pct.description: Precondition (b): dual-validator deviation rate during shadow mode, as a percentage. < 0.5 satisfies (b).

open_p0_count.description: Precondition (c) HARD: count of open P0 issues in the schema-revision-candidates queue (plan 048 § 14.A.3). Must be 0 to flip; the calendar ceiling never overrides this.

governanceSignoff.description: Precondition (d) HARD: the CTO + CISO + VP DevRel triple sign-off (plan 033 § 14.12). All three must be present for the advisory->blocking flip. STUB INTERFACE: the flip-emission code reads these three booleans + their reference fields; the upstream source of truth for a recorded sign-off is an ISEDC Decision Record (AT-DECR). Until a live governance-signoff source exists, this block is hand-maintained in SAK-STATE.json and the three booleans default false.

decision_record_ref.description: Pointer to the AT-DECR Decision Record recording the triple sign-off (e.g. '044-AT-DECR-...'). Required-by-convention once all three booleans are true; advisory otherwise.

transition.description: One recorded state transition. Append-only history; each entry records the from/to states, the trigger event, when it fired, and (for the flip) a reference to the governing Decision Record / Evidence Bundle.

event.description: The transition event name (e.g. 'wave-a-merged', 'enable-shadow', 'governance-triple-signs', 'regression-detected'). One of the events enumerated in scripts/sak-state-machine.py LEGAL_TRANSITIONS.

at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (date or date-time) the transition fired.

reason.description: Free-text rationale; required for ROLLED-BACK entries (plan 033 § 14.4.rollback records the reason in an AT-DECR-phase-4c-rollback DR).

decision_record_ref.description: Pointer to the kernel-side Decision Record this transition emitted (plan 033 § 14.4.shadow: 'Each transition emits a kernel-side Decision Record + Evidence Bundle entry.').

stateMachine.description: The SAK Phase-4 flip state machine. current_state must be one of the eight lifecycle states; states is the declared lifecycle ordering; preconditions carries the flip-gate inputs; transition_history is the append-only record of transitions taken.

entered_at.description: When current_state was entered (ISO date or date-time).

next_gate.description: Human-readable description of the next gate that must clear to advance.

states.description: The declared lifecycle states in nominal forward order. Every entry must be a valid lifecycle state; every state in current_state must appear here.

transition_history.description: Append-only record of transitions taken to reach current_state.

rollbackRecord.description: The SAK Phase-4c rollback record stamped by scripts/sak-kernel-gate-revert.py when a gate is reverted to ROLLED-BACK (plan 033 v7 § 14.4.rollback class 3 + plan 048 v8 § 14.4c). Present only after a kernel-gate-revert. Carries the governance-triple authorization, the revert reason/target, and the ISEDC Class-2 retrospective deadline (revert_at + 7 calendar days). The kernel-side Evidence Bundle 'rolled-back-superseded' superseding event is owned by the kernel emitter (F-MK-005); this record only carries the intent (affected_window + superseding_signing_mode), never a Rekor write.

reverted_at.description: ISO date or date-time the revert fired.

reason.description: Free-text rationale for the revert (required — plan 033 § 14.4.rollback records the reason).

reverted_gate.description: The gate that was reverted (e.g. 'validate-plugins.yml' flipped back to advisory mode).

decision_record_ref.description: Pointer to the AT-DECR-phase-4c-rollback-NNN Decision Record (plan 033 § 14.4.rollback class 3 (b)).

governance_signoff.description: The CTO + CISO + VP-DevRel triple that authorized this revert (all three true; the revert is refused otherwise).

retrospective_due_at.description: ISO date the ISEDC Class-2 retrospective must be opened by = reverted_at + 7 calendar days (plan 033 § 14.4.rollback class 3 (d)).

retrospective_window_days.description: The retrospective window in calendar days (7 per § 14.4.rollback class 3 (d)).

retrospective_class.description: The ISEDC retrospective class triggered by the rollback (Class-2 per § 14.4.rollback class 3 (d)).

affected_window.description: The BLOCKING-window the kernel-side emitter must mark 'rolled-back-superseded' (recorded intent only — NOT a Rekor write; the superseding envelope event is owned by the kernel emitter per F-MK-005).

superseding_signing_mode.description: The signing_mode the kernel emitter applies to affected_window attestations ('rolled-back-superseded'). Present only when affected_window is set.
